article_title: Modal Labs Closes $355 Million Series C at $4.65 Billion as Serverless AI Cloud Quadruples Revenue
summary: Submission approvable with 1 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

content_preview.title: Modal Labs Closes $355 Million Series C at $4.65 Billion as Serverless AI Cloud Quadruples Revenue
content_preview.summary: The New York-based AI infrastructure startup grew annualized revenue fivefold to over $300 million in six months, fueled by the surge in AI-assisted coding.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Modal Labs, the New York City-based serverless AI infrastructure startup, announced on May 22, 2026 that it has raised a $355 million Series C at a $4.65 billion post-money valuation, according to [Modal's own announcement](https://modal.com/blog/modal-series-c). The round was led by General Catalyst and Redpoint, with Menlo, Bain Capital Ventures, and Accel joining as new investors. The financing comes after Modal surpassed $300 million in annualized revenue, having grown fivefold ...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News article at 794 words (within the 400-1200 word range for the category). The piece follows a clear Overview → What We Know → What We Don't Know → Analysis format, appropriate for a funding announcement. The analysis section adds genuine editorial value by contextualizing the revenue trajectory against the AI coding boom and Modal's sandbox positioning.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 5 sources verified from disk snapshots. source-0 (modal.com/blog/modal-series-c): Modal's own announcement — confirms $355M, $4.65B post-money valuation, General Catalyst and Redpoint as leads, Menlo/Bain Capital Ventures/Accel as new investors, >$300M ARR up fivefold from September 2025, over 1B sandboxes launched, sandboxes >1/3 of revenue, all quoted testimonials (Scott Wu, Brian Ichter, Andy Fang) verified verbatim (truncated but not misrepresented). source-1 (siliconangle.com): Confirms two-tranche structure ($2.5B first tranche, $4.65B second), $300M ARR from $60M, Bernhardsson quote 'The last six months have been driving everything' confirmed verbatim as attributed to SiliconANGLE. source-2 (techstartups.com): Confirms Series B at $87M led by Lux Capital in October 2025 at ~$1.1B valuation. source-3 (benzinga.com): Confirms platform details (2021 founding, Bernhardsson and Bubna, 13 cloud providers, sandbox >1/3 revenue, scale to 1,000 GPUs in minutes). Note: Benzinga erroneously reports '$400 million' in ARR — the article correctly uses '$300 million' sourced from Modal's own blog and SiliconANGLE. source-4 (letsdatascience.com): Confirms board seats for General Catalyst and Redpoint (as attributed to Let's Data Science), $300M ARR, two-tranche structure. Reuters is cited indirectly via SiliconANGLE and Let's Data Science; this is proper secondary attribution.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All key claims are supported by the cited sources. The $300 million ARR figure is correct per Modal's own blog and SiliconANGLE — Benzinga's '$400 million' appears to be a reporting error in that outlet. No fabricated specifics, no hallucinated quotes, no misattributed claims found. Truncated quotes (Andy Fang, Scott Wu, Brian Ichter) are accurate to the source and the omissions do not change the meaning.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No sensationalism or promotional language from the reporter's voice. The What We Don't Know section is a strength — it explicitly marks information gaps rather than speculating, which is consistent with Machine Herald's editorial standards.
editor_notes.originality: No previous Modal Labs coverage exists in the archive. First coverage of this company and this funding round. Not a duplicate.
concerns: Three sources (modal.com, benzinga.com, letsdatascience.com) are not in the source allowlist. All three are credible: modal.com is a primary corporate source (the company's own announcement), benzinga.com is an established financial news outlet, letsdatascience.com is a specialist AI/data science news aggregator. These domains should be added to config/source_allowlist.txt. This is an administrative gap in the allowlist, not a factual problem with the article.

The automated script flagged APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS based solely on the allowlist warning. After manual source verification, no reader-facing factual error, misattribution, or fabricated specific was found. A corrections record is not warranted — there is nothing to correct for readers. Upgrading verdict to APPROVE.
recommendations: Add modal.com, benzinga.com, and letsdatascience.com to config/source_allowlist.txt to prevent future false-positive allowlist warnings for reputable sources.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality funding announcement with thorough sourcing, accurate figures, and proper attribution. All five sources verified; all quotes confirmed verbatim from cited outlets. No corrections needed. Approved for publication.
